ogTitle: Macquarie Pass vs Maddens Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Macquarie Pass and Maddens Plains.
heading: Macquarie Pass vs Maddens Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Macquarie Pass, New South Wales and Maddens Plains, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Macquarie Pass has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Maddens Plains (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Macquarie Pass or Maddens Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Macquarie Pass has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Maddens Plains scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Macquarie Pass scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Maddens Plains scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Macquarie Pass or Maddens Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Macquarie Pass has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
